description: Avoids repeated calls to `datetime.now()` by storing the timestamp in a variable.
rationale: Calling `datetime.now()` repeatedly creates multiple `datetime` objects, which is unnecessary. Storing the result in a variable and reusing it avoids this overhead.

changes.new:
now = datetime.now()
            self.kb.upsert(
                ids=[f"{topic}_{now.strftime('%Y%m%d%H%M%S')}"],
                documents=[doc_text],
                metadatas=[{"topic": topic, "timestamp": now.isoformat()}]
            )

changes.old:
report_id = f"integration_{topic}_{datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d%H%M%S')}"
            self.kb.upsert(
                ids=[report_id],
                documents=[f"Integration Report -- {topic}:\n{report}"],
                metadatas=[{
                    "topic": topic,
                    "type": "deep_knowledge",
                    "timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(),
                    "source": "cross_pollination"
                }]
            )
changes.new:
now = datetime.now()
            report_id = f"integration_{topic}_{now.strftime('%Y%m%d%H%M%S')}"
            self.kb.upsert(
                ids=[report_id],
                documents=[f"Integration Report -- {topic}:\n{report}"],
                metadatas=[{
                    "topic": topic,
                    "type": "deep_knowledge",
                    "timestamp": now.isoformat(),
                    "source": "cross_pollination"
                }]
            )
